title: Ofqual updates compliance policy for more agile regulation
body: "Ofqual has updated its policy to ensure high standards in qualifications.  The policy, titled ‘Supporting Compliance and Taking Regulatory Action’, introduces the new Chief Regulator’s Rebuke and supports agile regulation.  Sir Ian Bauckham CBE, Chief Regulator at Ofqual, said: ”This updated policy reflects our agile approach to regulation. We want to ensure awarding organisations resolve issues early wherever possible. However, when awarding organisations do not meet our high standards, we will use our enforcement powers in the interests of students and all who rely on qualifications.”  The policy includes a new tool called the Chief Regulator’s Rebuke. This new tool can be used when an awarding organisation is found to have breached rules, but not in a way that is serious enough to warrant a financial penalty.  Awarding organisations can appeal to Ofqual’s Enforcement Panel if they disagree with a decision that they have not met requirements, or if they receive a Chief Regulator’s Rebuke.  A rebuke will only be published after the appeal period has ended.  The updated policy also shows Ofqual’s commitment to early engagement, proportionate action, and transparency.  The policy does not introduce new requirements for awarding organisations.  Notes to editors  The full policy is available here: Supporting compliance and taking regulatory action.  The consultation analysis document is available here: Analysis: Supporting compliance and taking regulatory action.  The policy relates only to qualifications regulated by Ofqual and the awarding organisations that provide them.   This new policy follows 2 consultations in 2019 and 2025 and comes into immediate effect.   For media enquiries please contact the Ofqual press office on 0300 303 3014 or media@ofqual.gov.uk. "

taxons.title: Inspections and performance of education providers
